data.query: Botulinum toxin type A AND restless legs syndrome

results.title: Effectiveness and Safety of Botulinum Toxin Type A in Treatment of Restless Legs Syndrome: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
results.abstract: Our study aimed to investigate the effectiveness and safety of botulinum toxin type A in patients with restless legs syndrome. We searched electronic databases, including PubMed, Cochrane Library, and Web of Science, up to 12 June 2021, for published articles. We enrolled randomized controlled clinical trials and non-randomized controlled studies involving patients with restless legs syndrome who were treated with botulinum toxin. Quality assessment was performed using the Cochrane risk of bias tool and Joanna Briggs Institute Critical Appraisal Checklist for Quasi-Experimental Studies. As for the results, we included four articles comprising 62 participants, two studies were randomized controlled trials. Improvement in International Restless Legs Syndrome Study Group (IRLSSG) rating scale was observed in three studies. Adverse events were temporary and self-limited. Meta-analyses were performed, including the two randomized controlled trials with 27 participants. Compared with placebo

results.title: Botulinum Toxin in Restless Legs Syndrome-A Randomized Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Crossover Study.
results.abstract: Restless Legs Syndrome (RLS) is a common movement disorder with an estimated prevalence of up to 12%. Previous small studies with onabotulinumtoxin A (OnaA) for RLS have shown inconsistent results.

results.title: Successful treatment of recalcitrant restless legs syndrome with botulinum toxin type-A.
results.abstract: We report the effective use of injected BTX-A to treat refractory restless legs syndrome (RLS).
results.journal: Journal of clinical sleep medicine : JCSM : official publication of the American Academy of Sleep Medicine

results.title: Failure of botulinum toxin A to relieve restless legs syndrome.
Double-blind, placebo-controlled, pilot trial of botulinum toxin A in restless legs syndrome.
Pilot trial of onabotulinumtoxina (Botox) in moderate to severe restless legs syndrome.
results.abstract: To evaluate the potential of onabotulinum toxin A (Botox®, Allergan, Inc., Irvine, California) in the treatment of restless legs syndrome (RLS).

results.title: Restless legs syndrome (RLS) with expansion of symptoms to the face.
results.abstract: It has been well established that restless legs syndrome (RLS) manifests with symptoms most frequently in the legs followed by the arms. Other body parts may be affected but it has been somewhat controversial as to whether symptoms may extend to the face.

results.title: Botulinum toxin A: new hope for RLS?
results.journal: Journal of clinical sleep medicine : JCSM : official publication of the American Academy of Sleep Medicine
